description: This file contains Balanda present continuous modal constructions using 'nonve'. This modality expresses present ability or possibility in an ongoing action and corresponds to the English meaning 'can be ...-ing'.

rules.core_meaning: Present ability or possibility during an ongoing action

affirmative_form.note: This construction expresses positive modal meaning with continuous aspect

question_form.function: Forms yes/no questions expressing present continuous ability or possibility

constraints: nonve must immediately follow the subject
dho must appear between the modal and the verb
ti- must be retained on the verb
No future marker (le-) may appear in this construction
No negation marker (gba) appears in this file
No word order change occurs in questions
we? always appears at the end of the clause
This construction must not be confused with past continuous modality (nokanve + dho + ti-VERB)

balanda: Ne nonve dho tiyai a Wau
english: I can be going to Wau.

balanda: Ni nonve dho tidhii agbi tiyi
english: He can be coming to the house.

balanda: Wiki nonve dho tinono gbi difo
english: The child can be walking in the compound.

balanda: Ni nonve dho timande gbi njü
english: He can be working in the garden.
I can be knowing the truth.

balanda: Ni nonve dho tivel e takoni
english: He can be telling a story.
I can be writing a book.
He can be reading a book.
I can be buying a bike.
I can be pushing the fruit.
He can be pulling the drum.
He can be turning the table.

balanda: Ne nonve dho tikeye ti ndi

english: We can be discussing the story.
He can be hugging the child.
He can be protecting the child.

balanda: Ni nonve dho tisita gbi tiyi
english: He can be lying in the house.
I can be doing the work.

balanda: Ne nonve dho tiwala gbi Wau
english: I can be living in Wau.
I can be watching the animals.

balanda: Ne nonve dho tija nza we?
Ne nonve dho tinzü ngo we?
Ne nonve dho tiyai a Wau we?
english: Can I be going to Wau?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tidhii agbi tiyi we?
english: Can he be coming to the house?

balanda: Wiki nonve dho tinono gbi difo we?
english: Can the child be walking in the compound?

balanda: Ni nonve dho timande gbi njü we?
english: Can he be working in the garden?

balanda: Ne nonve dho timerego ŋo we?
Ne nonve dho tiŋiyi sereso we?
english: Can I be knowing the truth?

balanda: Ne nonve dho tiwe ndi we?
Ni nonve dho tivel e takoni we?
english: Can he be telling a story?

balanda: Ne nonve dho tibaha ŋo we?
Ne nonve dho tisudi kitab we?
english: Can I be writing a book?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tidheke kitab we?
english: Can he be reading a book?

balanda: Ne nonve dho tisïhï kese we?
english: Can I be buying a bike?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tisadi nza we?
Ne nonve dho tituru linguu we?
english: Can I be pushing the fruit?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tizaka kporo we?
english: Can he be pulling the drum?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tikara kafa we?
english: Can he be turning the table?

balanda: Ne nonve dho tikeye ti ndi we?
Danda nonve dho tiwofo takoni we?
english: Can we be discussing the story?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tikolo wiki we?
english: Can he be hugging the child?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tivanda wiki we?
english: Can he be protecting the child?

balanda: Ni nonve dho tisita gbi tiyi we?
english: Can he be lying in the house?

balanda: Ne nonve dho tima nde we?
english: Can I be doing the work?

balanda: Ne nonve dho tiwala gbi Wau we?
english: Can I be living in Wau?

balanda: Ne nonve dho tiduru ngo we?
Ni nonve dho tidüŋü kpendii we?
Ne nonve dho tiloro kananje we?
english: Can I be watching the animals?
